Hey everyone, this is Russell Brunson. I want to welcome you to week number one. As you know, this process is going to be going through four different weeks. We've got Hook's story offer and the funnel, but we're kind of flipping things backwards because we're going to start with the offer. We're going to go offer and then next week we'll go into story and then go into Hook's and then we're going to go into the funnel and traffic and things like that. So it's kind of backwards, but this is the foundation of how everything works. If you have a really, really good offer, then you can be bad at selling. You can have a bad story. All these other things will kind of make up for itself, but if you have a really good offer and a really good story and Hook and everything else talk about, then you'll make even more money. But that's kind of the process. So we're going to be focusing this week on really identifying and understanding and creating an irresistible offer that people have to give you money for. Now this first mission, should you choose to accept it, we're going to be talking about offer hacking and that's going to be the goal for this presentation is to help you understand the core concepts and your homework tonight is you're going to actually going to go and offer hack a bunch of different people. Now, I'm not talking about funnel hacking. I'm talking about actual offer hacking. You'll see the differences here in a few minutes. So that's kind of how I want to start this off. Now, before we get too deep into offer hacking, I need you to understand a couple core concepts that are super necessary. You understand these things originally came from the expert seekers book. Hopefully you've had a chance to read it. If not, you should make sure reading these books because a lot of the core concepts, I go a lot deeper into those inside the expert book expert secrets book and dot com secrets book. But the first thing we'll talk about because this is going to help you figure out a structure offer. A lot of times people come and they start with just like, this is what I'm going to sell and have like here's an offer and you want to you want to step back and really identify who your customer is first before you go into actually creating the offer. And I'll show you why here in an example actually, I believe tomorrow, you'll see a little more detail. But the first phase is understanding how the markets work. And so if you look at the way the market works right now, there's basically there's three core markets. You've got health, you've got wealth, and you have relationships. Now I call them three core markets. Stephen Larson sometimes has I've heard him call it the three core desires. So these are the markets or the desires, what people are looking for, what they actually want. Okay, they want to be healthier, they want to have relationships and they want wealth. And so when you understand this, no matter what it is you're trying to sell always fits somehow into one of these three categories. Even sometimes some products may be harder at first to understand you like how does this fit in? One good example, again, I heard Stephen share this, I'm sure he'll share with you guys in live coaching as well, but like razor blades, razor like where does this fit? This is a razor blade, it's not health, wealth or relationships. But think about it, what is the reason why you shave your face? What's the reason why you grow a beard? What's the reason why you try to look good in any fashion? Okay, why do you soap because you want to smell good? Like why do we care? Well, it's probably something to do with our relationships, right? I shave so I want to get closer to my wife or I grow a beard because I want women to come to me or whatever the thing is for you, right? And so, gelette razors would fit directly into relationships. Soap could fit into relationships or it could fit into health. You know, there's different places and so you got to understand that, that like any product or service you're selling fits somehow into one of these things, okay? And so that's the first phase. Now, if you were to re-wound backwards like, you know, 150 years ago before the TV and things like that, it was really simple. There weren't a lot of products out there on the market, things just kind of plugged in really, really easily. But unfortunately, or fortunately, depending how you're looking at it, the markets evolved, right? Back in the day, there was like, there was let's say there's one person teaching each of these things or selling things in these things, in each of these three core markets. And because of that, there wasn't a lot of competition, right? Like I remember back when I was a kid, it wasn't like there were 500 different razor companies. There was like gelette, like that was it, right? There's one company and it wasn't until the evolution of the markets and how things changed and how distribution became easier, where also more people could jump in, right? Because of the internet now, you got the dude who started Dollar Shave Club, who literally was able to start this business on a shoestring and grow it up and sell for over a billion dollars because the distribution was easier. He can make a funny video that went viral and so he was able to like get in front of people. We're back pre-internet, pre-TV. He wasn't able to do that. Does that make sense? You had to have a big budget to be able to do it. So back in the day, there were just a few core players. But as the world's evolved, where we are today, where we have easier distribution because the internet now all of us have a chance to play in these markets. And so initially, we all went in there and kind of competed the same thing, but it got harder and harder and harder. And so there might be 20 people teaching wealth and 100 people teaching health and relationships. So it got harder. So what happened then, the next evolution of this whole thing was people broke out of the markets into what I call submarkets. So instead of just like people teaching, I'm going to help you get wealthy, people started breaking that down and turning these into like subcategories or niches or whatever you want to call them. And so the subcategories, there could be a whole bunch of them. So like say inside a wealth, they could be like, I'm going to teach you real estate investing. And then under there, it could be, I'm going to teach you stock trading. That's the way to get wealthier. I'm going to teach you marketing. I'm going to teach you what other wealth things are. There's selling things on eBay. I'm going to teach you. And there's this thing after thing after thing. There's all these different submarkets that kind of broke out of the wealth. And so this made it, this opened up the horizon even bigger for all of us, which is really, really cool. And so that's kind of next thing, there's these submarkets. Now, what's interesting is you look at this, and this is kind of the world that, where things shifted to, but today's got even more, there's more competition, more things like that. And so it's actually gone one more level deep into what I call the niches. Okay, so you've got the three core markets or desires, and there's the submarkets and there's niches. So the niches, for example, is someone that say they pick real estate. This is the submarket I want to be in, but then they're like, man, there's like 800 real estate trainers. So how do I, how do I find my spot in the ecosystem inside of that? Right? And so from there, they come over to these niches. So like one guy was like, Oh, well, I'm going to teach people how to flip houses with no money down. And so I'm going to teach people how to do short sales. I'm going to teach people how to do. And so everyone had kind of had their own little, their own little spot inside the real estate ecosystem. Okay. And so most markets have been developed to the point where people are now in the niches. And so I want you to understand that because as you're creating your offers, you need to understand kind of where you're at. Now, if you read the expert secrets book and as we evolve things, probably not too much in this 30 day challenge, we evolve things, we're going to spend a lot of time figuring out like, how do you create your own new opportunity, your own new niche that's not the same as everybody else's? And so we'll get to more of that as we go deeper in the training. But right now, I just want you to be super aware of this is that the thing, these things are happening. So we understand it, then it's easier for us when we get in the offer hacking process to be able to find out what are other people doing who are like us. Okay. So now you kind of have a vision of this, what I want you guys doing is looking at this and thinking for yourself, okay, with the product or the service or the thing that I want to sell, where does it fit in here? Now, I know some of you guys are coming into this training with a product like this is my product, I've created it, or I wrote this book or I have a training course or I have a physical product I sell on Amazon or I'm in a network marketing program or whatever. Most of us have something. If you don't have something, my next question is like, which one of these things gets you most excited? Right? Are you excited about relationships, about health, about wealth? Like which one of these gets you the most fired up? And if you don't know what you want to sell yet, that's okay, pick which one of these gets you the most excited. Okay. Some of you guys I know aren't excited about health. Some of you guys are really excited about health. Some of you guys, you know, like where is your spot that you're actually excited about? Then come down here, then inside of that, that market inside the sub markets, what's the next thing you're most excited about? Like do you love real estate investing or stock markets or internet marketing? Right? What's the thing you're most excited about? And then inside of there, I'd start finding, I'd be finding a niche that you like. Now, initially, if you don't have a product, that's okay. What you can do is find somebody else who's already carved this one out and become an affiliate and sell their product. I don't care for this example, it doesn't matter to me as much. Okay. In fact, this whole 30 day challenge, I don't want people stressing out things. I'm trying to build this huge empire, this big company and I got to write the first time. Like a lot of you guys, if you don't know yet, just go through this 30 day process and just pick anything because we're just going to like, we're going to be teaching you all the skill sets, right? This is again, Mr. Miyagi, wax on, wax off, like paint the fence things. I'm just trying to teach you guys all the skill sets over 30 days. And for example, when I first got started in this business, who knows what my very first product was, was teaching people how to make potato guns. Now, if I just sat back and thought through it, I'm like, oh, potato guns are small market. How am I going to scale? How am I going to do blah, blah, blah? But luckily for me, I didn't. I just thought I just need to make some money. I want to try something. So I tried a thing. And what's cool is I learned how to do landing page. I learned how to do an upsell. I learned how to do an email sequence. I learned how to do copy. I learned how to get pages online. I learned how to buy domain. I learned all these different little things that you're going to have to do to be successful. And after I did it once for the potato gun, then I was like, oh, I know all the pieces to make this thing successful. And then I was able to go back and when I had my next idea, I was able to do it really fast because I'd done it once. Okay. So for you guys, don't stress out. It's easy to do a second time and a third time and a fourth time and, and continue to grow your company and your empire and whatever you want in the future. But right now, I just want you guys to understand the process when you guys waxing the fence, when you guys waxing the cars, painting the fences, right? So if you already have a product and you kind of know, okay, this is, this is where my people are at. That's the first step when you know, if you don't know yet, just figure out what I want to do. I'm excited about wealth. I'm excited about internet marketing, funnels. I'm excited about funnels. So if you're into funnels, cool, sell my stuff and you can do that during this process. If you're not excited about funnels, because you're excited about real estate, find somebody else that's got a real estate training program you like, it could be Robert Kiyosaki, it could be, I don't care who it is, almost everybody online has an affiliate program right now. Just pick something and that's what you need to do for phase one. Okay. All right. So you picked, you kind of know what it is, right? So that's the first step here is really understanding the markets. Now, the next thing that I want you to do is I need you guys to understand what are people selling right now? Okay. I'm a big believer in this concept called funnel hacking, right? We talked about it in the pre-training videos. It's like, I don't want to just start from scratch. I want to find out what are other people doing. I want to understand what they're doing and then we're going to model it, but then iterate upon it. Okay. Now, again, I say model, I want to stress this so many times. We're not talking about copying. Sometimes people go and they just knock things off. That's not what we're talking about. I'm looking at why is this dude or this lady right here in the market, why is she making tons of money selling that thing? Why? There's got to be a reason. I don't know what it is, but why is she making money or he making money? Why is this person making money? What have they already created? What have they already figured out inside of this market that we can look at and learn from? Okay. That's what offer hacking is about. What offers are currently working in the marketplace today? Okay. This is how we start this whole process. We don't start with like, what should I create? I'm going to make a thing, right? We find out what's currently selling. In fact, the other day we talked about the first rule of direct response marketing, which is a confused mind always says no. I'd say the second rule of direct response marketing is sell what people are already buying. Right? So you have to go and reinvent the wheel. It's figuring out what are they buying? Okay. People are buying software. They're buying training. You have to figure out what these guys have figured out people are buying and we want to understand that so I can create my own thing using this as a framework. Does that make sense? Okay. All right. So we're going to figure that out. So what I want you to do is we're going to kind of figure out where, like who some of these people are. So say, okay, I like Robert Kiyosaki, like Tony Robbins, I like Russell Brunson, like whoever it is and kind of make a list of some of these people. I'm going to show you in a minute how to see behind the scenes what's happening with all of their offers. Okay. Right now, I just want you to make a list of like who are some of these people you want to kind of spy on during this first assignment. Okay. So that's the first step of the process. Okay. Now what I'm going to do, I'm going to show them to map out kind of what's going to happen. I'm going to do it on the computer with you guys. So the second part of this now is what I'm going to do is I'm going to look, I'm going to spy behind the scenes and see exactly what they're doing. What's nice is because Facebook's recent issues with privacy policy, they've given us marketers the greatest gift in the world. They give us access to see everybody's ads and exactly what's working right now. And so because that makes this process really, really fun. So I'm going to kind of map out the goal with this and I'm going to actually log into Facebook and show you guys a bunch of different things. And I'm going to give you assignment based on that. Okay. So make sure you're paying a lot of attention right now. In fact, I'm going to move these books so I can show up to you in here a little better. So the first element all we're going to have is a hook. Number two is a story. And number three is an offer. Okay. So these are like the three core principles of everything you're going to do. Now, now that you guys are going to see that, you're going to see me doing it over and over and over and over again. I do this probably 50 times a day between the podcast I do, the emails I send out, the ads, the Facebook lives, the Instagram's that like every communication I'm doing, I'm looking at hook story offer, hook story offer. What are they? Where are they at? Okay. So I'm kind of like showing you guys behind the scenes. I want you to become super hyper aware of these three things. Hook, story offer. So what's the hook? Okay. The hook is the first thing where you're grabbing someone. Right. So someone's on Facebook, they're on their phone, they're scrolling through this like going crazy. They could be at their desk, it could be at school, they could be going to the bathroom, they could be walking, they could be on the plane, they could like anywhere, right? They're scrolling and your job is to set hooks that will grab somebody and stop them from scrolling and get their attention. That's what hook is. Going and grabbing someone and getting their attention. Okay. So hook could be a lot of things. Sometimes it's an image, right? Think about when you're scrolling, what grabs you. Sometimes it's an image, sometimes it's a headline, sometimes it's a picture, sometimes it's a video, like something that like stops your attention and hooks you. Okay. We're talking about that here in a minute, but the first step is, is figuring out the hook. Okay. Then the second thing is after you hook them, you got their attention, now you have a chance to talk to them, you're going to just tell them a story. Okay. And there's a lot of ways we'll get deeper next week into the whole story selling process. That's phase number two. And then third phase, we're going to be focusing on a lot today is, and this week is the offer. Okay. So here's exactly what I'm going to give you. Okay. So if you look at any, any of these funnels breakdown, anything, it's always hook. So we're grabbing their attention, telling them a story and then making them an offer. Hook, story, offer, hook, story, offer. Okay. So with your kids already sitting down and like when you're watching TV, ask them like, let's see commercial kids, what was the hook? What was the story? What was the offer? Hook, story, offer. If it's not there, means the ads probably not doing very well. Okay. If they are there, that's a good chance that's doing good. You have to develop a hook. If you don't have a hook yet, you're going to struggle, right? And so you have to develop hook. So for him, for Drew, he didn't have a hook, right? He was a trainer, just like every other trainer. And if you look at like, what's the number one question that trainers get all the time is like, well, of course, you know, of course you're in shape. Like you've always been in shape. You've never been fat. You don't know what's like. So Drew kept hearing that from his clients and was like, you know what? I'm going to, and he, I'm sure he wasn't like at the time thinking, I'm going to create a hook, but that's what he did. He said, well, I'm going to, I'm going to do this. I'm going to gain a bunch of weight. I'm going to go from being fit to fat to fit. And then, and then I'll know how I can serve my clients better. So I've gone through the process. So he started doing this and started gaining weight. He gained like 70 or 80 pounds through this process. But that hook was so good that when people heard about it, they shared with other people and shared with more people so much so he ended up on Jay Leno show, like two or three months in this process. So like, this is Drew from nowhere. He wasn't a big famous guy ahead of time. He was just a regular personal trainer who had such a good hook. They ended up on Jay Leno talking about it, gained a whole bunch of weight, and they lost so much weight later, right? So there's his hook. Okay. So for you, it's like, what's your hook? Don't pick just one guru. Like go pick 10 different businesses, 10 different brands, 10 things that you're interested in and go and search those things and try to find hook story at offer for all those things. I want you to start mapping them out. Okay. The main reason I want you to do this is because as you start doing this, it's going to get your marketing senses smarter. Right? You're going to get sharper. You're going to start seeing hooks and it's going to help you start developing your own hooks. Okay. That's the number one. Number two, you can start seeing how people are telling stories, which is going to help you to make, become better at telling stories. And number three, you can start seeing all these offers and it'll get you better at making offers. People always ask me now, how in the world does every offer you put out there successful? And the reason why is because I've spent the last decade and a half of my life making tons and tons and tons of offers. I know what offers don't work, but I also know what offers do work. I also look at all these different businesses, right? Like in fact, I was funnel hacking a weight loss company and I saw they had a recipe book and I was like, I should have a recipe book for my company. I'm selling funnels, but I want a funnel cookbook. So I made a funnel cookbook because I saw it in like every single weight loss offer that I funnel hacked, that I did this offer hacking for every single one of them has some kind of cookbook. I'm like, why don't I have a cookbook? I should really have a cookbook. So I put together an actual cookbook. Okay. If you look at, you know, marketing secrets, I'm trying to do an opt-in. I'm like, how did it, like if I want to get some of these email orders, I have to make an offer. What's the offer? And I came up with this idea for this ebook. And again, we'll talk about this in the further sessions in the future. But it's the same thing. Like I'm trying to create good offers, good hooks, good stories to get people to move through my funnel. Okay. Some people come to me like, oh, my funnel doesn't work. Russell, what's wrong? So I'm looking at, I'm looking at each step in the process. I'm looking at what's your hook is any good? What's your story is any good? What's the offer? And if nobody's clicking your ad, I'm like, okay, your hook sucks. We got to change it. Try another hook. I put out 10, 15, 20 hooks. When Anthony D. Commedy launched the biohacking secrets book, guess what? I told him, he had the funnel all built out and he said, okay, I'm going to start advertising this. I said, do a Facebook live every single day. So the first Facebook live he did, he put out a hook and guess what? Nobody came and bought it. I think it was like some technical thing like, hey, how to biohack your mitochondria or something weird like that. Right? He said the hook, nobody took it. The next day I said, do another Facebook live. He did another one. Nobody took it. Did another one. Few people took it. Did more and more. And after, I think it was like 10 or 11 times, like 11 times, he did a Facebook live that was how to biohack your vegetables. And guess what? That was the right hook. And then he made a video showing how to like cook your vegetables in organic grass-fed butter. And people saw that and that story resonated and said, hey, go get my free book. Click here. Hook story offer. Boom. That started working. That video, I'm getting over a million views. Then it came to the landing page. I was like, hey, I'm Anthony. Here's my book. Okay, there's a free hook book with like 500 different biohacks. There's the hook. They told the story. I've been biohacking my whole life. I've been doing these things in my body. Boom. There's a story. What's the offer? Get my free book. Hook story offer. Boom. Anthony, the company blew up. Okay. So, things don't work yet. I'm just looking at hook story offer. Hook story offer. Hook story offer. So, for you, you have to become like a scientist. You have to become like a spy out there looking at everybody else's offers. Okay. What a, if you ever wanted like what I do when I'm bored, if I'm at home alone or my wife's gone or like I have a free hour, I'm in Facebook looking at every fan page, looking at their ads and scrolling through them like, oh my gosh, that's such a good hook. Like, I'm not going to be able to copy Drew's hook, right? Because he went from fit to fat to fit. That's not my business. But like, could I take that hook? Could I tweak it for something in my business? Like, what if I went from fit to fat to fit? What if I went from, you know, from broke to rich to broke? You know, or if rich broke to, you know, whatever that is, maybe I go and I try to challenge like he did. Like, let me create a hook for mine or maybe I see that, you know, who knows, Dave Aspery did something or Jillian Michaels or just any of the brands I like out there, look at what they did and like, oh, how could I take that hook and tweak it for something I'm doing? Like, could I do something similar? Okay. And then look at how they tell the story and look how they structure their offer. Because now, in during this 30 day challenge, you're going to have to have a chance or you're going to be structuring an offer. And if you've seen 10, 20, 30, 40, 50 other offers, you're like, well, Russell, no one's buying my thing. What do I sell? What should I do? Like, you'll know exactly what to do. You're like, man, I look at 20 different offers in my marketplace and every single person is offering these five things. So I should definitely offer these five things. But I'm also going to add these things that are even better. And then I put my twist on it. And my, and my, how can I make it better and more intriguing and make the offer more, more valuable? Okay. And that's what I'm always looking at. It's funny because we talked about this in earlier videos. And I'll probably talk about it again and again, because it's so, so important. But there's two ways to make the thing that you're selling cheaper. Okay. There's two ways. And number one is what most people do and they create, they have a commodity. And so the only way to make a commodity cheaper is to lower the price, right? You lower the price. Okay. The other way to make this thing cheaper is to increase the value. Okay. When you increase the value of the offer, this seems cheaper. Okay. So for you, it becomes a master that either I have to lower my price and become the lowest price leader in town and have a horrible business where I'm miserable and broke all the time. Or I figure out how to create an amazing offer. So the value goes up, which actually makes this cheaper, right? Cause I'm just selling you a book, you know, if I'm just selling you a thing, an e-book is only worth 20 bucks. Like, like, that's the value. But if I say you get the e-book, plus you get the restaurant guys, you get, all these things, suddenly the value, each thing is, keeps going up, going up, going up. And suddenly, like, this becomes, this thing's really, really cheap because the value got so high. Okay. So that's the two different things you're able to do to increase the value. And again, we'll get deeper into this, but that's the way you guys are understanding. So your Simon for tonight and for this week, really why you're focusing on you and get your kids and your family, everyone involved, go to Facebook, find the brand you like, start looking at their ads and have some fun diagrams for at least 10 different offers. And I'd recommend at least one of them you buy and look at the upsells as well, just so you can start seeing behind the scenes of the funnels. But do this and figure out hook story offer for each of the ads. Hook story offer for whatever page you land on. If it's an opt-in page, you go to the next page and hook story offer for the sales page. Go as deep as you're able to go and look for hook story offer, hook story offer, hook story offer and start writing those things out and then start focusing in on the offer. Cause this is what we're going to be creating this week. You have to get super good at creating offers. We're going to show you how to quickly, how to do it fast, how to create amazing offers. But this is the most important thing you can be doing right now is just getting a good understanding of the offers that are out there. We talked about this multiple times. Direct response. You got to sell one of the direct response rules. Are you going to sell what people are buying? So see what people are already buying and then we're going to take that as a framework and improve upon it and make our offers even better. And so that's it for the step. You guys do your homework assignment, have some fun with it, enjoy it and with that said, we'll see you guys on the next video.
